title: Banking-metric knowledge registry
description: Meta-knowledge about banking metrics: for each metric, whether it is disclosed at all, in a standardized way across banks, on a regular cadence, and whether WE can reproduce it from the data we hold (esp. the BRSA quarterly audit reports in bank_audit_*). This is the ontology above docs/METRICS.md (sector metric definitions) and web/app/lib/chart-specs.catalog.json (specific reproduced charts). See docs/BANKING_METRICS.md.

axes.description: Human-readable glossary of each classification axis and its allowed values (kept in sync with the enums below; scripts/metric_knowledge.py asserts parity).

formula.description: Concise math expression, distinct from `definition` (prose) and `derivation` (how to get it from OUR data). e.g. 'net_income / avg_equity'.

frameworks.description: Definitional origin: basel_iii (capital/liquidity ratios), ifrs9 (staging/ECL), tfrs (Turkish IFRS accounting line), brsa (BDDK-defined regulatory ratio), market (equity-market metric), management (bank-defined, non-standard), none.

decomposes_into.description: Metric ids this one breaks into (decomposition tree, e.g. roe -> [roa, equity_multiplier]). Each must be a known metric id.

related.description: Loosely related metric ids (drivers, numerator/denominator, peers). Each must be a known metric id.

level.description: The unit of observation. bank = a single bank; group = a BDDK bank-type aggregate; sector = whole banking system; macro = economy/central bank.

availability.description: How/whether the metric is disclosed. mandatory = required regulatory disclosure (BRSA/BDDK financial statements & tables); voluntary = management-disclosed in IR/earnings decks, definition set by the bank; third_party = published by an external provider (rating agency, TBB); none = not publicly disclosed.

cadence.description: Reporting frequency. adhoc = published irregularly / only when the bank chooses; none = not a time series.

standard_across_banks.description: Is the metric defined consistently enough across banks to compare like-for-like? Regulatory line items: true. Management metrics whose definition varies by bank (e.g. 'active digital customers', a bespoke market-share peer set): false.

reproducible.description: Can WE reproduce it from the data we currently hold? direct = a stored field; derived = a formula/transform over stored fields; partial = only approximable, or only at the wrong level/cadence; no = not from our data.

source_datasets.description: Which of our datasets carry (or would carry) this metric. bank_audit = per-bank BRSA quarterly statements; bddk_monthly/weekly = sector bulletins; evds = TCMB macro; tbb_digital = TBB sector digital stats; external = needs a source we don't ingest; none = unavailable.

reproducible_from_audit.description: Convenience flag: true iff this can be reproduced (direct/derived) specifically from the bank_audit_* reports. Derived/checked by scripts/metric_knowledge.py.

derivation.description: How to compute it from our fields (hierarchy codes / sections / transforms).

spec_ids.description: chart-specs.catalog.json ids that operationalize this metric, if any.

ours.description: The value we compute from our data, for cross-check.
